LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT VALERA BARNHART'S DEMURRER TO THE SEVENTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF TRENT JASON IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. MR. JASON HAS NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGED THAT HE JUSTIFIABLY RELIED ON MS. BARNHART'S ALLEGED FRAUD OR THAT THE ALLEGED FRAUD RESULTED IN DAMAGES. HE HAS ALSO NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGED ANY FACTS TO SUPPORT HIS NIED CLAIM AGAINST MS. BARNHART. MR. JASON HAS TWENTY DAYS TO FILE A SUCCINCT, NO MORE THAN THIRTY PAGE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT REMOVES ALL LEGAL ARGUMENTS AND ONLY ALLEGES, WITHOUT REPETITION, THE ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING THE ELEMENTS OF EACH HIS CLAIMS. (CCP 425.10(A) ("A COMPLAINTSHALL CONTAINA STATEMENT OF FACTS CONSTITUTING THE CAUSE OF ACTION, IN ORDINARY AND CONCISE LANGUAGE")). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600 =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CSAA INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. AND CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH THROUGH EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR UNFAIR COMPETITION, MISREPRESENTATION, FALSE PROMISE, DECEIT, AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF TRENT JASON IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION. AS FOR THE UNFAIR COMPETITION CLAIM, THE SAC FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT CSAA ENGAGED IN UNFAIR, DISHONEST, DECEPTIVE OR FRAUDULENT PRACTICES WITH INTENT TO DEFRAUD THE PUBLIC IN VIOLATION OF CIVIL CODE 1711. AS FOR THE MISREPRESENTATION, FALSE PROMISE, AND DECEIT CLAIMS, THE SAC DOES NOT ALLEGE ADEQUATE FACTS SHOWING WHO MADE WHAT MISREPRESENTATIONS OR FALSE PROMISES, AND WHEN, HOW AND TO WHOM SUCH MISREPRESENTATIONS OR FALSE PROMISES WERE MADE. THE SAC ALSO FAILS TO PLEAD FACTS THAT SHOW THAT MR. JASON ACTUALLY AND JUSTIFIABLY RELIED ON CSAA'S ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION OR FALSE PROMISE TO HIS DETRIMENT AND AS A RESULT, INCURRED ACTUAL DAMAGES. THE NIED CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE BAD FAITH, NOT NEGLIGENCE, IS THE SOLE REMEDY AGAINST INSURERS LIKE CSAA FOR DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF ALLEGEDLY MISHANDLED CLAIMS. AFTER BEING GIVEN AN OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE VIABLE CLAIMS, IT APPEARS THAT MR. JASON IS NOT ABLE TO DO SO. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. =(302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Bad Faith Actions Or Tactics Pursuant To Civ. Proc. Section 128.5
Off Calendar Oct-24-2019 Off Calendar

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Bad Faith Actions Or Tactics Pursuant To Civ. Proc. Section 128.5
Off Calendar Oct-24-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AMERICAN AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Sanctions For Bad Faith Actions Or Tactics Pursuant To Civ. Proc. Section 128.5.

Defendant American Automobile Association of Northern California, Nevada & Utah's motion for sanctions for bad faith actions or tactics is denied.

Defendant asserts that whether Plaintiff has engaged in "bad-faith actions or tactics that are frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay" (Code Civ. Proc. 128.5(a)) is "measured by the objective reasonable attorney standard." (Mot. at 6.) Defendant is wrong. Section 128.5 was amended in 2017 to specifically overrule San Diegans for Open Government v. City of San Diego (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 1306, which held that "an objective standard applies when determining whether a party's or an attorney's conduct is sanctionable under section 128.5." (In re Marriage of Sahafzadeh-Taeb and Taeb (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 124, 128.) That legislation clarified that " 'the standard applied in Section 128.5 is subjective bad faith.'" (Id. at 134.) Thus, the 2017 legislation returns the meaning of "bad faith" under section 128.5 to the majority view prior to San Diegans, which " 'require[d] a showing not only of a meritless or frivolous action or tactics, but also of bad faith in taking the action or tactic.'" (Id. at 133-135.)

In addition to relying on the wrong legal standard, Defendant also asserts that Plaintiff should be sanctioned because he has appealed from the Court's ruling granting its motion for summary judgment. (Mot. at 2, 4, 6.) Defendant urges the Court to grant its motion "to prevent further abusive tactics directed toward both defendant and the legal system." (Id. at 6.) That request is improper. The Court will not award sanctions to deter a party from filing an appeal that it has a right to prosecute, and the merits of which the Court will not (and cannot) prejudge. If Defendant believes that Plaintiff's appeal is frivolous, it may seek appropriate relief from the Court of Appeal. (See In re Marriage of Flaherty (1982) 31 Cal.3d 637, 650-651.)

Defendant is correct in asserting that "[w]hen a tactic or action utterly lacks merit, a court is entitled to infer the party knew it lacked merit yet pursued the action for some ulterior motive." (Dolan v. Buena Engineers, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1500, 1505.) "But it also 'is within a court's discretion not to draw that inference if convinced the party was acting in the good faith belief the action was meritorious.'" (In re Marriage of Sahafzadeh-Taeb and Taeb, 39 Cal.App.5th at 144, quoting Dolan, 24 Cal.App.4th at 144.) Here, Defendant "points to no evidence which conclusively demonstrates [Plaintiff] pursued [his] action against it in bad faith." (Dolan, 24 Cal.App.4th at 1505.) The Court granted summary judgment for Defendant based on a finding that Plaintiff failed to present any evidence to support his claims, and rejected his various theories as unsupported by controlling law. Nevertheless, it cannot conclude that Plaintiff necessarily acted in subjective bad faith, however misguided or even foolish his theories may be. Nor does it see any basis for ordering a self-represented litigant to pay a $24,000+ sanctions award, which can have no practical benefit and will only multiply the litigation that Defendant claims it wishes to avoid.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lisa A. Costello, Esq. of Michael Tran Goldberg & Costello, 925.279.4232, appearing by CourtCall for the Defendant CSAA Insurance Service;
No appearance by any party or counsel.

Matter called.

There being no other appearance, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS CSAA INSURANCE SERVICES, INC. AND CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE, INC. NOTICE OF DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT; DEMURRER is adopted as follows: Defendants CSAA Insurance Services, Inc. and CSAA Insurance Exchange's demurrer to the fourth through eighth causes of action for unfair competition, misrepresentation, false promise, deceit, and negligent infliction of emotional distress in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Trent Jason is sustained without leave to amend as to all those causes of action. As for the unfair competition claim, the SAC fails to allege facts showing that CSAA engaged in unfair, dishonest, deceptive or fraudulent practices with intent to defraud the public in violation of Civil Code 1711. As for the misrepresentation, false promise, and deceit claims, the SAC does not allege adequate facts showing who made what misrepresentations or false promises, and when, how and to whom such misrepresentations or false promises were made. The SAC also fails to plead facts that show that Mr. Jason actually and justifiably relied on CSAA's alleged misrepresentation or false promise to his detriment and as a result, incurred actual damages. The NIED claim fails because bad faith, not negligence, is the sole remedy against insurers like CSAA for damages arising out of allegedly mishandled claims. After being given an opportunity to allege viable claims, it appears that Mr. Jason is not able to do so. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Lisa Costello, Esq. of Carbone Smoke Smith Bent & Leonard, T:510.267.7222, for defendant/moving party, by Court Call;
Trent Jason, T:707.984.6570, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is: a) overruled as to the first, second, and third causes of action for breach of contract, breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, and insurance bad faith for failure to properly investigate a claim; and b) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth, sixth, and seventh causes of action for misrepresentation, false promise, fraud, and negligent infliction of emotional distress. CSAA's argument that it is not the insurance company that issued the automobile insurance policy is not amenable to resolution at the pleading stage. Plaintiff Trent Jason adequately pleads the legal effect of the contract in order to apprise CSAA of the claims being made against it and does not need to set out verbatim the terms of the contract. Mr. Jason also pleads sufficient facts alleging breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing and insurance bad faith for failure to properly investigate a claim. Mr. Jason has not adequately alleged justifiable reliance, a required element for all of his fraud claims, and is given leave to do, if he can do so in good faith. Mr. Jason has also not adequately alleged that CSAA acted negligently and is given leave to amend to correct that deficiency.

Counsel for CSAA is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.
